 美国参议院商业，科学和运输委员会通过了修订，没有任何辩论，将NASA变化添加到无尽的前沿法案，这是一项使我们与中国和其他国家竞争激烈的法案。
 后来在听证会期间，随着条例草案的其他修正案被辩论和拒绝，印第安纳州的共和党托德年轻人的无尽的边境法案联合赞助商之一，表达了对该过程的挫败感。 ＆＃34;本条例草案，在主要，不应该是空间，私人空间公司，＆＃34;年轻人说。 ＆＃34;它在主要是关于竞争，出竞争，外出，外交的共产主义中国。＆＃34;
  令人堵塞的康无息情况和美国宇航局修正案附属于无尽的前沿法案，这是一个可能会通过国会搬迁的最终立法车辆，强调其紧迫性作为坎迪尔维尔·罗杰柳条的坎迪斯皮和共和党的优先事项。从挖掘一点进入文本，它＆＃39;不难看出原因。
  立法要求为NASA的额外资金拨打10.03亿美元，以执行人类登陆系统计划。这种立法是蓝色的，杰夫·贝罗斯一直敦促国会增加了100亿美元的美国宇航局的预算 - 足够的金钱，以全面基于第二个人着陆系统的发展。
 美国宇航局确实希望为月球兰德开展竞争，但它只选择了一家公司 -  4月中旬在国会大会中仅在2021年预算中拨款少量资金。 SpaceX＆＃39;总投标只有29亿美元，不到其竞争对手的一半，包括蓝色源地LED国家队。 ＆＃34;我们看着政府的最佳价值，＆＃34;当时的美国宇航局人类勘探计划的主任说凯西·吕德斯。 
康斯威尔州和第39条修正案要求美国宇航局向两名着陆机构前进。如果修正案签署了法律，美国宇航局可能必须重新开放竞争，从而延迟原子能机构的工作＆＃39;返回月球并将已经困难的目标延迟到了2024年进一步的危险。
 本立法也忽略了NASA＆＃39;自己计划创造了一个农历兰德竞赛，并保持了2024年追踪的可能性。在美国宇航局＆＃39; S Spacex下，SpaceX将全速朝着2024年的登陆工作，而第二家公司将被带入争夺后续的着陆。但这并没有堵塞迎来。随着她的修正案，康普尔似乎会说，如果蓝色起源可以＆＃39; t被列入该计划，Artemis应该向前推进。
  该法案还要求NASA继续开发＆＃34;勘探上阶段，＆＃34;这是该机构的新，更强大的第二阶段和＃39; S空间发射系统火箭。此外，该法案表示，这一上级应准备好用于火箭的第三次推出。
 这项要求的一个问题 -  NASA表示，它不需要勘探上阶段来完成其早期的Artemis Moon任务。第一次发射将使用市售的上阶段，这足以推动宇航员的船员，以便将猎户座的航天器到月球。
  因此，这种立法使美国国家航空航天局负担了上阶段的发展 - 可能会花费大约100亿美元，并在原子能机构正忙于完成第一个月亮任务时需要五年的时间。虽然他们不会公开承认，但一些美国宇航局工程师甚至不确定他们需要上阶段。如果Spacex＆＃39; S Starship车辆成功，它将更强大，成本更高，而且比SLS火箭更频繁地飞行，即使是其先进的上阶段。
 由于公司拥有普遍合同，看看这项立法要求背后的波音之手是不是太难。
  这项立法中还有另外一个新颖的方面，真正驾驶其宫殿弯曲。康沃尔柳条修正案说美国宇航局应该＆＃34;启动用于空间发射系统的集成核心阶段推进元件的主要推进试验文章的开发，符合成本和安排限制，特别是对于飞行所需的长引进推进硬件。＆＃34; 
那么这是什么？它基本上是SLS火箭＆＃39;核心阶段的测试物品。美国宇航局没有要求的这种元素将有效地允许NASA和波音在绝望的密西西比州斯坦尼斯航天中心对SLS原型进行测试。
  这项提案的背溯是，密西西比人参议员柳条，不幸的是，美国宇航局只计划执行一系列＆＃34;绿色奔跑＆＃34;在第一核舞台上的Stennis测试。 （美国宇航局在3月完成了这个核心舞台测试，自从将第一个SLS火箭发布到佛罗里达州以来）。单一测试，原子能机构推出，足以验证该技术。
 然而，通过这种立法，美国宇航局可以做更多的测试，保持柳条＆＃39; S中心完全聘用和额外雇用的本地承包商。然而，它是否会推出美国宇航局的勘探努力，这是一个更具可疑的主张。
  美国立法过程并不简单，并不保证克里斯特和柳条＆＃39;修正案将成为法律。修订后的无尽的边界行为现在将转到完整的参议院，这种修正案可能会被剥夺。然后，账单必须去美国房子，在那里它将全额考虑，并且再次将从立法中删除修正案，因为它并不是特别是杰明。
 即使该法案成为法律，这秋季或冬季也可以通过拨款过程来取代。这是＆＃34;授权＆＃34;立法。为了支付第二次兰德，国会预算作家将不得不通过拨款过程实际资助。在此过程中，参议员可以通过资金，而不是人类着陆系统来覆盖这种语言。
 无论如何，截止口修正案发出了关于美国参议院的几个基本事实。首先，某些成员对NASA的决定非常不满意，决定为月球兰的巨大合同提供了巨大的合同。其次，他们仍然选择狭隘的政治，在NASA说它实际上需要回到月球上。
note_en:
On Wednesday, a US senator added an amendment to unrelated science legislation that would impose significant restrictions on NASA and its plans to return to the Moon.
 The amendment ( see document) was spurred by NASA's decision in April to select SpaceX as its sole provider of a human landing system for the Artemis Program. Senator Maria Cantwell, a Democrat from the state of Washington, where Blue Origin is based, authored the legislation. Owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, Blue Origin led a lunar lander bid that was rejected by NASA.
 The US Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation passed the amendment without any debate, adding the NASA changes to the  Endless Frontier Act, a bill to keep US scientific and technology innovation competitive with China and other countries.
 Later during the hearing, as other amendments to the bill were debated and rejected, one of the Endless Frontier Act co-sponsors, Republican Todd Young of Indiana, expressed frustration with the process. "This bill, in the main, is not supposed to be about space, private space companies," Young said. "It in the main is supposed to be about competing, out-competing, out-innovating, outgrowing communist China."
  That Cantwell's NASA amendment was attached to the Endless Frontier Act, the first available legislative vehicle likely to move through Congress, underscores its urgency as a priority for Cantwell and a Republican from Mississippi, Roger Wicker. From digging a little deeper into the text, it's not difficult to see why.
  The legislation calls for $10.03 billion in additional funding for NASA to carry out the Human Landing System program. This legislation comes as Blue Origin and Jeff Bezos have been urging Congress to add $10 billion to NASA's budget—enough money to fully fund the development of a second Human Landing System.
 NASA did indeed want to have a competition for the lunar lander, but it  selected only one company—SpaceX—in mid-April after Congress only appropriated a small amount of money in the fiscal-year 2021 budget. SpaceX's total bid was only $2.9 billion, less than half that of its competitors, including the Blue Origin-led national team. "We looked at what’s the best value to the government," said Kathy Lueders, chief of the human exploration program for NASA at the time.
    Cantwell's amendment mandates that NASA move forward with two landers. If the amendment is signed into law, NASA would likely have to re-open the competition, thus delaying work on the agency's return to the Moon and putting an already difficult target of 2024 into further jeopardy.
 This legislation also ignores NASA's own plans to both create a lunar lander competition as well as keep the possibility of a 2024 landing on track. Under NASA's plans, SpaceX would work at full speed toward the 2024 landing, while a second company would be brought on to compete for subsequent landings. But this has not mollified Cantwell. With her amendment, Cantwell seems to be saying that if Blue Origin can't be included in the program, Artemis shouldn't move forward.
  The bill would also require NASA to continue development of the "Exploration Upper Stage," which is a new, more powerful second stage for the agency's Space Launch System rocket. Moreover, the bill says this upper stage should be ready for use on the third launch of the rocket.
 There's just one problem with this requirement—NASA says it doesn't need the Exploration Upper Stage to complete its early Artemis Moon missions. The first launches will use a commercially available upper stage, which is powerful enough to launch a crew of astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft to the Moon.
  This legislation therefore burdens NASA with the upper stage development—likely to  cost about $10 billion and take five years—at a time when the agency is busy enough trying to complete the first Moon missions. And although they will not admit this publicly, some NASA engineers are not even sure they need the upper stage. If SpaceX's Starship vehicle is successful,  it would be more powerful, cost less, and fly more frequently than the SLS rocket, even with its advanced upper stage.
 It is not too difficult to see the hand of Boeing behind this legislative requirement, as the company has the prime contract to develop the Exploration Upper Stage.
  There is one other novel aspect of this legislation that really drives home its parochial bent. The Cantwell-Wicker amendment says NASA should "initiate development of a main propulsion test article for the integrated core stage propulsion elements of the Space Launch System, consistent with cost and schedule constraints, particularly for long-lead propulsion hardware needed for flight."
    So what is this? It's basically a test article of the SLS rocket's core stage. Such an element, which NASA has not asked for, would effectively allow NASA and Boeing to perform tests on an SLS prototype at Stennis Space Center in Southern Mississippi in perpetuity.
  The backstory to this proposal is that Wicker, the Mississippi senator, is not happy that NASA only planned to perform a series of "Green Run" tests at Stennis on the very first core stage. ( NASA completed this core stage test in March and has since shipped the first SLS rocket to Florida). A single test, the agency reasoned, would be enough to validate the technology.
 With this legislation, however, NASA could do more tests, keeping Wicker's center fully engaged and local contractors gainfully employed. Whether it would advance NASA's exploration efforts, however, is a far more dubious proposition.
  The US legislating process is not straightforward, and there is no guarantee that Cantwell and Wicker's amendment will become law. The amended Endless Frontier Act will now go to the full Senate, where this amendment may get stripped out. Then the bill must go to the US House, where it will be considered in full, and again the amendment may be removed from the legislation, as it is not particularly germane.
 And even if the bill becomes law this fall or winter, it still could be superseded by the appropriations process. This is "authorizing" legislation. To pay for a second lander, congressional budget writers will have to actually fund it through the appropriations process. During this process, senators could override this language by funding,  or not, the human landing system.
 Regardless, the Cantwell amendment signals a couple of basic facts about the US Senate. First of all, some its members are really unhappy with NASA's decision to give SpaceX a large contract for a lunar lander. And secondly, they're still choosing parochial politics over what NASA says it actually needs to return to the Moon.
title: 在Spacex Moon Lander奖之后，国会在NASA发出警告
title_en: Congress fires warning shot at NASA after SpaceX Moon lander award

不披露交易术语。根据节点的数据，ZeeEkit在外部资金筹集了超过2400万美元，但我们已确认这是不准确的。 2016年，Zeekit筹集了900万美元的900万美元系列，自2014年以来总计1600万美元。
 该公司已经在收购情况下，包括一系列零售商和品牌，包括沃尔玛，以及梅西，ASOS，Tommy Hilfiger，Adidas等。它曾经在时装周期间与Rebecca Minkoff一起工作，以帮助女性店铺的外表。
  ZeeEkit于2013年由首席执行官Yael Vizel，VP的研发Nir Appleboim和CTO Alon Kristal成立，前提是，如果在线购物者可以看到衣服如何看待自己的身体，该技术可以降低到期的回报率不适合，不讨人喜欢的物品。
  沃尔玛表示，客户将能够使用Zeequit技术，包括免费的人，冠军，Levi的施特劳斯，Eloquii元素，自由装配，索菲亚牛仔裤，索菲亚vergara，加上其自己的私人标签品牌，以及其自身的私人标签品牌，以及时间和TRU，Terra＆amp;天空，奇迹国家和乔治。
 当技术在Walmart.com上生活时，客户可以选择上传自己的图像或从一系列最能代表其高度，形状和肤色的模型上传，以便在任何衣服中看待自己。目标是在网上购物时提供类似的经验，因为在零售店时，你会在网上购物时。
 通过新的集成，购物者还能够与朋友分享他们的虚拟服装，并通过新的集成，将社会元素添加回在线购物。
 除了虚拟尝试之外，沃尔玛还表示，Zeekit的技术可用于建立其他时尚体验，包括虚拟壁橱体验，您可以在那里混合和匹配样式。 
随着交易的关闭，Zeekit的三个联合创始人将加入Walmart。
 “我们相信，随着团队在为时尚世界带来实时形象技术，计算机视觉和人工智能的专业知识，我们将确定更多为我们的客户提供创新的方式，以便成为第一个 - 选择目的地的时尚目的地，“沃尔玛Incandela，沃尔玛·美国博览会的服装和私人品牌，在一个公告中。
 多年来沃尔玛经过大量投资服装，包括收购Bonobos，Modcloth，Eloquii等的在线品牌，甚至尝试提供一些品牌，如耐克，他们在沃尔玛自己的商店。 COM。并非所有这些努力都已退还。沃尔玛在购买它后几年只售出Modcloth，例如，在由零售巨头归所拥有的Modcloth客户之后，品牌仍然无利可图。最近，沃尔玛与在线寄售商店的合作牌，在沃尔玛的网站上列出了大量的二手项目。
 除了盈利能力周围的斗争外，服装更广泛地是在线零售的艰难地区，往往是因为挑选了挑选了独特的身体的物品和非标准尺寸时装设计师使用的困难可以越来越小或更大，取决于给定的品牌，即使在购物“你的尺寸”时也是如此。
 可能影响收购的另一个因素是大流行，这是推动电子商务年的未来，因为零售商关闭了他们的门，消费者留在卫生危机的情况下在线购物。在此期间，亚马逊通过沃尔玛作为美国的顶级服装零售商，根据威尔斯法戈的说法，其服装和鞋类销售额在2020年以上增长15％，超过410亿美元，或比沃尔玛高20-25％。
 沃尔玛没有说ZeeEkit将在沃尔玛的网站上生活，只有它会出现“很快”。
 我们了解到，收购后，Walmart不会继续运行Zeekit的现有业务。 ZeeEkit将在过渡计划上与当前客户合作。 
Updated 5/13/21，11:05 AM ET更准确的资金总额。 以前我们注意到了节点数据。 我们已经直接确认了这些数字。
note_en:
Deal terms were not disclosed. According to data from Pitchbook, Zeekit had raised over $24 million in outside capital, but we’ve confirmed that’s inaccurate. Zeekit  raised a $9 million Series A in 2016, and has raised a total of $16 million since 2014.
 The company had already been working with a range of retailers and brands ahead of the acquisition, including Walmart, as well Macy’s, Asos, Tommy Hilfiger, Adidas, and others. It had once  worked with Rebecca Minkoff during Fashion Week to help women shop the show’s looks.
  Zeekit had been founded in 2013 by CEO Yael Vizel, VP of Research and Development Nir Appleboim and CTO Alon Kristal, with the premise that if online shoppers could see how clothing would look on their own bodies, the technology could reduce the rate of returns due to non-fitting, non-flattering items.
  Walmart says customers will be able to use the Zeekit technology to virtually try on items brands including Free People, Champion, Levi’s Strauss, ELOQUII Elements, Free Assembly, Scoop, Sofia Jeans by Sofia Vergara, plus its own private label brands, like Time and Tru, Terra & Sky, Wonder Nation and George.
 When the technology goes live on Walmart.com, customers can choose to upload an image of their own or choose from a series of models that best represent their height, shape and skin tone in order to see themselves virtually in any item of clothing. The goal is to provide a similar experience to trying on clothing when shopping online as you would otherwise have had when in a retail store.
 Shoppers will also be able to share their virtual outfits with friends for a second opinion, via the new integration, adding the social element back into online shopping.
 In addition to the virtual try-on, Walmart says Zeekit’s technology may be used to build other fashion experiences over time, including a virtual closet experience where you could mix and match styles.
 With the deal’s closure, Zeekit’s three co-founders will be joining Walmart.
 “We’re confident that with the team’s expertise in bringing real-time image technologies, computer vision and artificial intelligence to the world of fashion, we’ll identify even more ways to innovate for our customers in our continued effort to be the first-choice destination for fashion,” said Denise Incandela, Walmart U.S. EVP of Apparel and Private Brands, in an  announcement.
 Walmart in years past had heavily invested in apparel, including by acquiring online brands like  Bonobos, ModCloth, Eloquii, and others, and even tried offering some brands, like  Nike, their own shop on Walmart. com. Not all of these efforts paid off.  Walmart sold ModCloth only a couple of years after buying it, for example, after ModCloth customers balked at being owned by a retail giant, and the brand remained unprofitable. More recently, Walmart  partnered with online consignment shop ThredUP to list a large number of secondhand items on Walmart’s website.
 In addition to the struggles around profitability, apparel more broadly been a harder area for online retail to get right, often because of the difficulties involved with picking out items that have to fit unique bodies and the non-standard sizing fashion designers use — meaning clothing can run smaller or larger, depending on given brand, even when shopping “your size.”
 Another factor that may have impacted the acquisition was the pandemic, which pushed e-commerce years ahead, as retailers closed their doors and consumers stayed home to shop online due the circumstances of the health crisis. During this time,  Amazon passed Walmart as the top apparel retailer in the U.S., according to Wells Fargo, which estimated its apparel and footwear sales grew 15% in 2020 to over $41 billion, or 20-25% higher than Walmart.
 Walmart didn’t say when Zeekit would go live on Walmart’s website, only that it would show up “soon.”
 We understand that, post-acquisition, Walmart will not continue to operate Zeekit’s existing business. Zeekit will work with their current customers on a transition plan.
 Updated 5/13/21, 11:05 AM ET with more accurate funding totals. Previously we noted Pitchbook data. We’ve since confirmed the figures directly.
title: 沃尔玛收购了虚拟衣服的启动Zeequit
title_en: Walmart acquires virtual clothing try-on startup Zeekit
